requirements: Applicants must hold a strong grade in data structures and algorithms and demonstrate solid Java programming including collections generics and basic complexity analysis. You should be comfortable leading a lab section of up to twenty students and explaining asymptotic notation tree traversals and basic graph algorithms on the board. Experience with Git version control and JUnit-style testing is expected. You will prepare short exercise sheets before each session and help students debug logic errors patiently. English fluency for classroom discussion is required and prior tutoring experience is a plus.
additional_notes: Lab sessions run Tuesdays and Thursdays from 3:00 PM to 5:00 PM in Building 3 Room 201. Arrive fifteen minutes early to prepare the room and verify projector access. Optional online office hours Wednesday 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM. Expect roughly eight hours per week including preparation and short grading of attendance checkpoints.

requirements: We seek TAs who mastered procedural and object-oriented basics in Java or Python and who can explain variables control flow functions and simple debugging strategies to beginners. You should be able to walk through compiler error messages and help students structure small assignments without writing code for them. Patience with diverse backgrounds and clear spoken English are essential. Experience with an IDE such as IntelliJ or VS Code is helpful. You may also assist with grading rubrics for weekly quizzes under the instructor supervision.
additional_notes: Drop-in hours are Monday Wednesday Friday 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM in the open lab on the second floor. You may split shifts with another TA. Typical workload is six to seven hours weekly including a short sync meeting on Monday mornings.

requirements: Candidates should have completed a team software project using Git branching code reviews and lightweight agile practices. You will mentor student teams on requirement clarification UML sketches and integration testing. Familiarity with Spring Boot or similar frameworks is welcome though not mandatory. Strong written feedback on pull requests and respectful communication in group channels are critical. You must document recurring issues for the instructor weekly.
additional_notes: Studio blocks are Monday and Wednesday evenings from 6:30 PM to 9:00 PM. Room capacity is limited so please enforce sign-in sheets. This posting is now closed for new applications after the department finalized staffing.

requirements: Applicants should understand IPv4 addressing subnetting TCP versus UDP basics and simple socket programming at least at an introductory level. You will supervise hardware labs configuring small LANs and running packet capture exercises with Wireshark. Ability to explain common troubleshooting steps in English is required. Shell scripting for automation is a bonus. Safety awareness when handling rack equipment is mandatory and training will be provided in week one.
additional_notes: Labs meet Friday afternoons 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM. Network closet access requires card access which TAs receive after training. Estimated weekly hours are seven including setup and teardown time.

requirements: We need one TA with strong competitive programming background including dynamic programming graph algorithms and careful complexity analysis. You will run weekly problem sessions and review student submissions for edge cases. Familiarity with online judges and C++ or Java for fast prototyping is expected. You should be able to present solutions clearly on a whiteboard and encourage collaborative pair programming during practice.
additional_notes: Workshops were held Saturday mornings 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM. This cycle has ended and the position is closed; remaining applications are archived for reference only.

requirements: have knowleges of java,have patience ,have experiences better.

Applicants must be reliable, detail-oriented, and committed to upholding exam integrity. Responsibilities include monitoring student behavior (e.g., preventing unauthorized materials, maintaining quiet), distributing/collecting papers, recording attendance/absences, and reporting irregularities (cheating, technical issues) to the chief proctor. Strong communication skills (fluency in English/local language for instructions) and calm demeanor in disruptions are essential. Prior proctoring or educational experience is preferred; training on exam protocols (e.g., emergency procedures) will be provided. Ability to arrive 30 minutes early for shift briefings and adapt to variable schedules is critical.
additional_notes: Arrive 30 mins early for setup/briefing. Optional debrief meetings post-exam week.
